body_txt: A Stock Phrase that's usually worded as "I'm an X, not a Y", with X being their profession, and Y being whoever would be better able to do the job that they are having difficulty with. The most famous example, and thus the Trope Namer , is Star Trek 's Dr. McCoy — but the earliest example we have so far is from 1869, making it not only Older Than Star Trek but Older Than Radio . (It was in 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea .) Note that DeForest Kelley, contrary to popular belief , never prefaced his statement with "Damn it, Jim" or some variant thereof; he never swore on the show, given that it aired in The '60s . He did swear up a storm in most of the movies, however, so parodists naturally combined his potty mouth with his catchphrase, to great effect. (He never actually used his catchphrase in a movie until the reboot — wherein he did swear, but he wasn't talking to Kirk, so the complete memetic phrase ultimately wasn't uttered until Star Trek Beyond .) Unrelated to Dr. Placeholder McDoctorate .
